verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its canonical localization is inner-leaflet/cytoplasmic — the kinase domain, SH2, and SH3 domains all face the cytosol. However, multiple lines of evidence support cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display: tumor cells (colon, breast, other cancers) have been shown to expose SRC-family kinases on the extracellular face of the plasma membrane (cancer ecto-kinase biology), where it has been targeted by surface-directed therapeutic strategies. This fits the 'cell_state_induced / oncogenic transformation' bucket. Under baseline non-transformed conditions, SRC is inner-leaflet anchored and not accessible from outside the cell. The documented cancer-cell ecto-pool elevates the verdict from 'no' to 'contextual'.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and wash-resistance of the cancer-cell ecto-SRC pool is not fully characterized; baseline biology is clearly inner-leaflet-anchored, and the ecto-display evidence is less robust than for canonical cancer ecto-chaperones like HSP90.
